stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags such as 'transformers' and 'llama'.
The content pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in generative AI tasks.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model belongs to the Llama family, indicating its connection to Meta's large language model ecosystem.
It is categorized under text-generation, signifying its primary function as a generative AI tool.

stories.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Elon Musk has sued OpenAI, alleging the company has deviated from its founding mission.
The lawsuit claims OpenAI is prioritizing profit maximization over its original goal of benefiting humanity through AI.
The high-stakes court battle could significantly impact the future direction and governance of OpenAI and ChatGPT.

stories.title: Elon Musk tried to hire OpenAI founders to start AI unit inside Tesla
stories.author: George Hammond, Financial Times
bullet_points: Elon Musk attempted to recruit OpenAI founders to establish an internal AI division within Tesla.
Musk's proposal included a for-profit structure contingent on him gaining control of the new unit.
The story highlights the intense competition and strategic maneuvering among major tech players in the AI space.

stories.title: TSMC taps wind power as AI chip demand soars, Taiwan feels energy crunch
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: TSMC is integrating wind power into its operations to meet surging energy demands.
The increased energy consumption is directly linked to the soaring global demand for AI chips.
The situation highlights a critical energy crunch facing Taiwan's advanced semiconductor manufacturing sector.

stories.title: Design Conductor 2.0: An agent builds a TurboQuant inference accelerator in 80 hours
stories.author: David Chin
bullet_points: Introduces 'Design Conductor 2.0,' an advanced multi-agent system capable of autonomous hardware design.
The system successfully built a TurboQuant inference accelerator in just 80 hours.
Demonstrates a significant leap in LLM agent capability, handling 80x larger and higher-quality tasks compared to previous work.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-R1 model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and utilizes transformers and safetensors formats.
It is associated with the deepseek_v3 family, indicating relevance within advanced LLM development.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads for Apple hardware.
This is presented as a preview, indicating ongoing development and performance improvements.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core feature emphasizes running AI entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run advanced coding models (like Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is collaborating with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open-source safety tools.

stories.title: ChatGPT’s ‘Trusted Contact’ will alert loved ones of safety concerns
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: OpenAI is introducing an optional 'Trusted Contact' feature for ChatGPT.
This feature allows users to designate contacts who will be alerted if the AI detects discussions of self-harm or suicide.
The tool aims to enhance user safety and provide a lifeline for mental health concerns.

stories.title: Low-Cost Black-Box Detection of LLM Hallucinations via Dynamical System Prediction
stories.author: Mohamed Akrout
bullet_points: Proposes a novel, low-cost method to detect LLM hallucinations by modeling the LLM as a black-box dynamical system.
Avoids computationally expensive traditional methods like sampling or external knowledge retrieval.
The technique involves projecting LLM responses into a high-dimensional manifold to analyze vector sequence realizations.

stories.title: The Impossibility Triangle of Long-Context Modeling
stories.author: Yan Zhou
bullet_points: Identifies a fundamental, provable trade-off in long-context modeling for sequence models.
The 'Impossibility Triangle' dictates that models cannot simultaneously optimize for Efficiency, Compactness, and Recall.
Formalizes this trade-off using an Online Sequence Processor abstraction, unifying various model architectures (Transformers, SSMs, etc.).

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under the 'text-generation' pipeline, indicating a focus on generative AI.
Associated technologies include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors, pointing to standard modern NLP tooling.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for advanced AI features within Claude Code.
The new capabilities include the implementation of subagents for complex task decomposition.
Web search functionality has been added, enhancing the model's ability to access real-time information.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to run tools like Claude Code with open-source models via Ollama.
It bridges the gap between proprietary LLM APIs (Anthropic) and the open-source ecosystem (Ollama).

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives like gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: SpaceX is starting to move on from the world's most successful rocket
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: SpaceX is beginning a transition away from its most established launch operations.
Vandenberg Space Force Base in California is slated to become a primary, high-volume launch site for SpaceX.
The news signals a potential strategic shift in SpaceX's launch infrastructure utilization.

stories.title: Anthropic raises Claude Code usage limits, credits new deal with SpaceX
stories.author: Samuel Axon
bullet_points: Anthropic is increasing usage limits for its Claude Code model.
The company has secured a new partnership with SpaceX.
This deal aligns with Anthropic's growing enterprise adoption alongside Microsoft and Amazon.

stories.title: Taming Outlier Tokens in Diffusion Transformers
stories.author: Chen Wei
bullet_points: The research investigates 'outlier tokens' within Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) used for image generation.
Outlier tokens, characterized by high norms, are shown to exist in both the encoder and denoiser components of RAE-DiT pipelines.
This work explores how these tokens, which carry limited local information, disproportionately attract attention in generative models.

stories.title: LongSeeker: Elastic Context Orchestration for Long-Horizon Search Agents
stories.author: Siheng Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of managing rapidly growing working contexts in long-horizon search agents.
Proposes an adaptive context management strategy where different parts of the agent's trajectory are maintained at varying levels of detail.
Introduces 'Context-ReAct' as a method to operationalize this principle, aiming to reduce computational overhead and errors.

stories.title: When Life Gives You BC, Make Q-functions: Extracting Q-values from Behavior Cloning for On-Robot Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Thomas Weng
bullet_points: The paper addresses a key limitation of Behavior Cloning (BC) in robotics: the lack of online self-improvement after initial demonstrations.
It tackles the problem of policy degradation in offline-to-online learning caused by distribution mismatch.
The proposed algorithm, Q2RL, integrates Q-estimation and Q-gating to enable efficient reinforcement learning from BC.

stories.title: Geometry-Aware State Space Model: A New Paradigm for Whole-Slide Image Representation
stories.author: Fei Xia
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of analyzing Whole-Slide Images (WSIs) in histopathology, which are gigapixel-resolution tissue scans.
It proposes a 'Geometry-Aware State Space Model' as a new paradigm for representing these complex images.
The work aims to improve upon existing Multiple Instance Learning (MIL) methods used for slide-level predictions.

stories.title: Aes3D: Aesthetic Assessment in 3D Gaussian Splatting
stories.author: Weidong Cai
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in evaluating 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) beyond mere reconstruction fidelity.
Proposes 'Aes3D' to assess higher-level aesthetic attributes like composition and visual appeal in 3D scenes.
Highlights the current limitation of existing 3D scene evaluation methods.

stories.title: Taming Outlier Tokens in Diffusion Transformers
stories.author: Chen Wei
bullet_points: The research investigates 'outlier tokens' within Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) used for image generation.
It addresses the known issue from Vision Transformers (ViTs) where high-norm tokens carry limited local information.
The study demonstrates this outlier token phenomenon exists in both the encoder and denoiser components of RAE-DiT pipelines.

stories.title: Estimating the expected output of wide random MLPs more efficiently than sampling
stories.author: Paul Christiano
bullet_points: The paper proposes a novel method to estimate the expected output of wide Random MLPs without requiring any sampling.
The technique leverages mathematical tools like cumulants and Hermite polynomials to approximate activation distributions layer-by-layer.
This offers a potentially more efficient alternative to traditional Monte Carlo sampling methods for calculating expected loss in ML.

stories.title: Understanding In-Context Learning for Nonlinear Regression with Transformers: Attention as Featurizer
stories.author: Rongjie Lai
bullet_points: The paper investigates In-Context Learning (ICL) in the context of nonlinear regression, moving beyond existing linear model theories.
It focuses on understanding how transformers achieve ICL—learning from prompt examples without weight updates.
The research specifically examines the attention mechanism as the key component ('featurizer') enabling this capability in nonlinear settings.

stories.title: Superposition Is Not Necessary: A Mechanistic Interpretability Analysis of Transformer Representations for Time Series Forecasting
stories.author: Alper Yıldırım
bullet_points: Investigates how Transformer models, popular in NLP, function when applied to time series forecasting.
Uses Mechanistic Interpretability (specifically Sparse Autoencoders) to probe the internal representations of these models.
Addresses the ongoing debate regarding the performance of Transformers versus simpler linear models like DLinear in time series tasks.

stories.title: What Matters in Practical Learned Image Compression
stories.author: Oren Rippel
bullet_points: The research addresses the gap between theoretical potential and practical application in learned image compression.
Learned codecs offer a key advantage over traditional methods by allowing direct optimization for the human visual system.
The study focuses on modeling choices for a practical learned codec optimized for both perceptual quality and runtime efficiency.

stories.title: The First Token Knows: Single-Decode Confidence for Hallucination Detection
stories.author: Mina Gabriel
bullet_points: Introduces 'first-token confidence' ($\phi_{first}$) as a novel, potentially more efficient method for detecting AI hallucinations.
Addresses limitations of existing self-consistency methods, which require multiple decodes or complex semantic clustering.
The technique leverages the normalized entropy of top-K logits at the initial token to gauge model certainty.

stories.title: Beyond Semantics: An Evidential Reasoning-Aware Multi-View Learning Framework for Trustworthy Mental Health Prediction
stories.author: Mengling Feng
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of deploying LLMs for mental health prediction in real-world, high-stakes settings.
Proposes an Evidential Reasoning-Aware Multi-View Learning Framework to improve trustworthiness.
Focuses on overcoming limitations like overconfident predictions and lack of reliable uncertainty estimation in noisy data.

stories.title: Continual Knowledge Updating in LLM Systems: Learning Through Multi-Timescale Memory Dynamics
stories.author: Constantine Dovrolis
bullet_points: Proposes a novel approach to continual knowledge updating in LLMs by mimicking biological memory dynamics.
Challenges current explicit external memory management systems by advocating for adaptive, multi-timescale memory.
Introduces 'Memini,' an associative memory framework designed to allow new associations to be immediately usable.

stories.title: Automatically Finding and Validating Unexpected Side-Effects of Interventions on Language Models
stories.author: Xiaoli Fern
bullet_points: Introduces an automated, contrastive evaluation pipeline for auditing LLM behavior.
The method compares generations from a base model ($M_1$) and an intervention model ($M_2$) across aligned prompts.
It generates human-readable, statistically validated hypotheses describing behavioral differences between models.

stories.title: The Pinocchio Dimension: Phenomenality of Experience as the Primary Axis of LLM Psychometric Differences
stories.author: Marcin Moskalewicz
bullet_points: Researchers analyzed 50 LLMs using 45 psychometric questionnaires to map their internal differences.
The primary axis of variance separating LLMs relates to 'phenomenally rich experience' (e.g., embodied sensation, empathy).
This contrasts with a secondary axis of 'stimulus-driven behavioral reactivity,' suggesting a dimension beyond mere input-output processing.

stories.title: DeepSeek 4 Flash local inference engine for Metal
stories.author: tamnd
bullet_points: DeepSeek has released a new local inference engine, 'DeepSeek 4 Flash'.
The engine is specifically optimized for Apple's Metal framework.
This facilitates running large language models locally on Apple hardware.

stories.title: Chrome removes claim of On-device Al not sending data to Google Servers
stories.author: newsoftheday
bullet_points: Google Chrome is modifying its claims regarding On-device AI processing.
The change suggests that previously stated data privacy guarantees for on-device AI might be inaccurate.
This development is significant as it touches upon the growing tension between AI capability and user data privacy.
